projects.name: Creating a neural network from scratch
projects.description: Implemented a Deep Operator Network (DON) from scratch. DON is a neural network architecture designed to learn mappings between function spaces. The project was done in Python and using NumPy.
projects.longDescription: DON is particularly suited for solving problems involving parameterized differential equations or operator learning tasks, where the goal is to predict outputs given input functions and parameters. Traditional methods like finite elements might be compotationally expensive, we leverage the fruits of Machine Learning, neural networks specifically, so develop an approximation of the desired function. Once well trained, DON provides fast predictions for new inputs with high accuracy. We evaluated this method by fitting the Poisson 2D function.
projects.personalTake: This was a course project but I truly found this project to be fun and educationally beneficial. While nowadays one can easily use libraries like PyTorch to implement a neural network in few minutes, I like to unravel the black box and dig my hands into it, and this project allowed me to do so.

projects.name: Nonlinear classification using nanoneuron
projects.description: Trained a surrogate model of a nanoneuron using Monte Carlo simulation method. Examined nonlinear classification capabilities of the nanoneuron model.
projects.longDescription: An investigation of the capability of the Dopant Network Processing Unit (DNPU) as a innovative candidate for neuromorphic computing. Since the fabrication of these units are expensive, this project leveraged Monte Carlo simulation methods and ML techniques to analyze its potential without physical fabrication.
projects.personalTake: This was my Bachelor thesis supervised by prof. Bernard Geurts. It was one of my first close encounters with Machine Learning and I loved it. Not just learning a lot about neural nets, but also the fun of research if done right. I was pretty lost sometimes, but I guess it is a part of the journey. I contacted series of researchers and students who participated in this research, their insights defninitely helped shape my today's direction of going into research as well.

projects.name: Real time point-to-point Measurement in 2D
projects.description: Developed on OAK-D camera using depthAI, openCV. Application also trackes pinned points with CSRT.
projects.longDescription: The application calculates the distance between two points in real time using the intrinsic matrix K and depth information at each pixel. The application also tracks pinned points with CSRT tracker. To make the tracking robust, a combination of spatial constraints and depth continuity is incoporated.
projects.personalTake: I see this more of a high level software that could have wide range of usecase in real life. I had fun experimenting different object tracking algorithms from openCV, none of them seem to be close to perfect though, especially when frame moves too which.

projects.name: Hospital Queue Simulation
projects.description: Simulated a hospital queue system with Python, applying mathematical analysis to suggest improvements.
projects.longDescription: Simulated a hospital queue system with different types of patients, arrival and service distributions. Analyzed the system using mathematical models and simulation results to propose enhancements. Explored various queuing strategies and evaluated their impact on patient wait times and resource utilization.
projects.personalTake: Although I learned a lot during this project, the structure made by the proffesors was not very clear, therefore I often had to intepret the questions by myself. But I also think this is a scenario that could happen in real life cases, where clients can give vague objectives and it is up to me to shape the problem to align with the client's goals effectively. Regarding the project itself, I really enjoyed making simulation and incorporating queueing theory to analyse the systems.

projects.name: Graph Isomorphism
projects.description: Implemented a graph isomorphism algorithm in Python to find isomorphisms between graphs.
projects.longDescription: Developed a graph isomorphism algorithm in Python capable of determining whether two graphs are isomorphic. Utilized advanced techniques, including fast partition refinement and branching, to efficiently find and validate isomorphisms. Conducted extensive testing and evaluation on diverse graph datasets to assess the algorithm's accuracy and performance.
projects.personalTake: What a project! I loved this project. Definitely had something to do with the fact that I was very invested into the problem of graph isomorphism. During this project, effecient techniques with branching, data structures played a huge part. I also started coding in vim during this period of time, and made a hilarious bug of leaving out a return line with a wrong number of tabs that took me hours to debug, as I said, hilarious.

projects.name: Portfolio
projects.description: Designed and developed a personal portfolio website using vanilla JavaScript, SCSS and HTML.
projects.longDescription: Designed and developed a personal portfolio website using vanilla JavaScript, SCSS and HTML. Implemented a responsive design that adapts to different screen sizes and devices.
projects.personalTake: I feel like coding this website was so much easier compared to when I was doing a similar project two years ago. It took me about 4 days to complete this website, then I took some time to ask for peers reviews and finalized it. There is still bunch of stuff I want to implement but this is presentable for now.

projects.name: Water Level Prediction
projects.description: Predicted water levels in Westerschelde using Python and Fourier transform for data analysis.
projects.longDescription: Implemented a linear regression model in Python to predict water levels in Westerschelde based on historical data. Employed Fourier transform to convert time-domain data to the frequency domain for spectral analysis. Conducted thorough evaluation and validation of the prediction model using appropriate performance metrics.
projects.personalTake: I was left behind during this project. I was not able to contribute as much as I wanted to, the people I worked with did most of the job very quickly and I was not aware of this. This did not mean any bad intentions from the colleagues, rather they were very motivated to do it and unfortunately, we did not communicate very well. On the other hand, I understand how 'deep' can you fall into the rabit hole once you start working on something on your own, I am no exception to this phenomenon. Next time, I will just make sure I am in the picture and initiate the communication. Luckily, my friends are extremely talented, so the project went very well.

projects.name: Visol (ships schedule)
projects.description: Designed and coded a ship container schedule using SCSS and JavaScript.
projects.longDescription: Developed a comprehensive ship container scheduling system using SCSS for styling and JavaScript for interactivity. Designed an intuitive user interface that enables efficient management of container arrivals, departures, and transfers. Implemented advanced scheduling algorithms to optimize container allocation and minimize operational costs.
projects.personalTake: I learned so much during this project regarding collaboration. We worked in a team of 5 people. I was in the team of frontend with a very experienced colleague in this area, so I learned a lot from his coding style and I think it really helped me have a better grasp of 'structured code'.

projects.name: Multi-hop ad-hoc chat
projects.description: Design and implement a fully distributed multi-hop ad-hoc chat application using emulated wireless communication.
projects.longDescription: Developed a distributed multi-hop ad-hoc chat application using Java. Simulated wireless communication between devices to enable chat messaging in a decentralized network. Implemented routing algorithms and message forwarding protocols to ensure reliable message delivery across multiple hops. Conducted extensive testing to evaluate the system's performance, scalability, and resilience to network disruptions.
projects.personalTake: Learning about networks and protocols is actually very interesting. I learned a lot about how the internet works and how the data is transferred. I also learned about the different protocols and how they are used in different scenarios. Designing our own protocol was challenging and increadibely fun.

projects.name: Pentago Game
projects.description: Implemented a two-player board game with game logic, server/client system, and an AI player.
projects.longDescription: Developed the Pentago board game in Java, featuring game logic, multiplayer functionality, and an AI player. Implemented network communication for multiplayer mode and developed an AI player capable of providing challenging gameplay. Conducted thorough testing and optimization to ensure smooth gameplay experience.
projects.personalTake: This project did not require a lot of logical thinking, it rather trained my understanding of client server methods and how to implement them. The game itself was fun to play though.

projects.name: Topdown Game on Raspberry Pi
projects.description: Coded an obstacle-avoiding topdown game in Python for Raspberry Pi.
projects.longDescription: Developed an engaging topdown game using Python specifically for Raspberry Pi. Utilized Pygame library for graphics rendering and user input handling. Implemented game mechanics such as obstacle avoidance and scoring. Presented the final game on a Raspberry Pi device with a small screen and joystick for an immersive gaming experience.
projects.personalTake: I have used Raspberry Pi before and getting one for free was one of the things I loved about this project. We had a lot of fun working on this project with friends. We used the pygame library to code this game so it was very easy.
